```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  pa <- sample(seq(0.20,0.80,0.05),1); pb <- sample(seq(0.20,0.80,0.05),1)
  ans <- pa*pb; vals <- round(c(ans,pa+pb,pa/pb,min(pa,pb),1-ans),4)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Eventos $A$ e $B$ são independentes, com $P(A)=`r pa`$ e $P(B)=`r pb`$. Quanto vale $P(A\cap B)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
A independência implica
$$P(A\cap B)=P(A)P(B)=(`r pa`)(`r pb`)
=`r sprintf("%.4f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S4Q5_criterio_independencia
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
